channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: BP Exits Bay du Nord Project offshore Canada
item.description: 'The transaction is consistent with BP's continued focus on portfolio simplification and disciplined capital allocation'.

item.title: North America Finally Snaps Rig Count Addition Streak
item.description: North America's rig count stayed flat week on week, according to Baker Hughes' latest North America rotary rig count.

item.title: Shell Expects Gas Trading Results to Exceed Q1
item.description: Shell expects trading results for its Integrated Gas segment to be 'significantly higher' for the second quarter compared to the prior three-month period.

item.title: Invictus Energy awards contracts for Musuma-1 site preparation
item.description: Invictus Energy has awarded several contracts related to the preparation and construction of the Musuma-1 exploration well within the Cabora Bassa Project in Zimbabwe.

item.title: German Stockpiling Agency Says It's Refilling Strategic Reserve
item.description: Germany's oil stockpiling agency EBV is looking to refill strategic reserves of diesel.

item.title: LNG Made Up 45 Percent of EU Gas Imports in 2025
item.description: LNG's share of annual European Union gas imports increased by nine percentage points to about 4.63 trillion cubic feet, 'deepening the structural shift in the EU's gas supply mix', the European Commission said.

item.title: Oil Market is Attempting a System Reboot
item.description: J.P. Morgan analysts, including J.P. Morgan's Head of Global Commodities Strategy, Natasha Kaneva, said the oil market is 'attempting a system reboot'.

item.title: The rise of alternative fuels: can biofuel feedstocks ever meet demand?
item.description: Offering decarbonisation for hard-to-abate industries, biofuel demand is increasing, but feedstock availability threatens to cap growth.

item.title: Geopolitical Risk Returns as Drone Strikes Hit Hormuz Shipping
item.description: Drone strikes in the Strait of Hormuz lifted oil prices as geopolitical risks resurfaced, even as weak physical crude markets continue to weigh on sentiment. Saudi Arabia Slashes Its Way Back Into Asia - Saudi national…

item.title: Ugandan Farmers Sue to Block $5 Billion East Africa Oil Pipeline
item.description: A group of Ugandan farmers on Tuesday filed a case in the UK High Court against the developer of a $5-billion oil pipeline in East Africa, seeking to stop the nearly completed project on environmental grounds. The $5…

item.title: Bangchak Completes Acquisition of Chevron Hong Kong
item.description: The Thai group said it had completed the purchase of Chevron's fueling business in Hong Kong, though acquired stations continue to offer the Caltex brand for two years.

item.title: Six Key Issues to Watch at NATO’s Ankara Summit
item.description: NATO leaders are meeting in the Turkish capital, Ankara, for the alliance’s annual summit on July 7-8 in the shadow of the Iran war and American complaints that not all European allies are pulling their weight when it…

item.title: ADNOC and Inpex sign 15-year LNG sales deal for Ruwais project
item.description: Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC) has finalised a 15-year sales and purchase agreement (SPA) to supply one million tonnes per annum (mtpa) of liquefied natural gas (LNG) to Inpex from the Ruwais LNG project.

item.title: Ontario, Alberta unveil proposed Hardisty-to-Sarnia oil pipeline route
item.description: Ontario and Alberta have outlined the proposed route for the Northern Shield Energy Corridor, a plan centred on a new crude oil pipeline intended to connect Hardisty, Alberta, to Sarnia, Ontario.

item.title: India Could Return To Iranian Crude If U.S. Extends Sanctions Waiver
item.description: Indian state-held refiners are considering buying Iranian crude oil if the United States extends the waiver on Iran’s oil sales beyond August or further eases restrictions on that trade. As part of the 14-point…

item.title: Gasoline Sees 3rd Most Expensive Independence Day Ever
item.description: 'Average gasoline prices fell in nearly every state over the last week...pushing the national average to approximately $3.74 per gallon this July 4', Patrick De Haan, Head of Petroleum Analysis at GasBuddy, said.

item.title: UAE Oil Output Climbs After Leaving OPEC
item.description: The United Arab Emirates raised ‌its crude output to near record highs above 3.8 million barrels per day in June, after the Gulf nation quit OPEC to escape production caps, Reuters reported citing two sources familiar…

item.title: Germany Plans $1.7 Billion Strategic Natural Gas Reserve
item.description: Germany is moving to create a state-owned strategic emergency reserve of natural gas to boost its energy security, the Economy Ministry of Europe’s biggest economy said on Tuesday. Earlier today, Reuters reported that…

item.title: Equinor to acquire bp’s stake in Bay du Nord offshore Canada
item.description: Equinor has signed an agreement to acquire BP’s non-operated stake in the Bay du Nord project located in the Flemish Pass basin, about 500km offshore Newfoundland and Labrador in Canada.

item.title: ADNOC Signs 15-Year LNG Supply Deal With Japan’s Inpex
item.description: Abu Dhabi’s national oil company ADNOC on Tuesday signed a 15-year long-term deal to supply gas from the UAE’s Ruwais LNG project to Japanese firm Inpex starting from 2028. Under the deal, Inpex, Japan’s largest…
